opp.name: Olvi_ANA,Intake,S2C
opp.next_step: Has to build out Business Case wtih internal Champion
opp.ais_why:
Procurement lead set a personal AI mandate: 'we have set also a personal target for this year to increase AI usage' and 'our whole leadership team is getting... AI mentoring.'
Explicit pull for agentic/AI: 'I would be really, really interested of the... sourcing agent... as well as the... Merlin insights'; on agentic sourcing, 'No. I think it... could even work.'
Commercial intent shown—'How much does it cost?'—plus a free POC offer and a value/ROI workstream. Two transcribed calls support high confidence.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 18 Jun 2026
Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026, speaker Suvi Lehtonen
Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026, speaker Ossi Heikkinen
metrics.narrative: Business problem confirmed: manual, day-old spend reporting built in Excel and PowerPoint, causing inefficiencies and version control issues; quarterly and monthly category reports consume significant time without real-time visibility. Quantified pain stated by Ossi on 26 Mar: 'if we send some report out and we have put the numbers and our things to the Powerpoint in last week and this week, we will present that... everything has been changed already' — the lag makes reports obsolete on delivery. Suvi added: 'we are responsible for making several types of reports... we don't even get it readily into Powerpoint format... it's print screens or taking Excels out and building the crafts ourselves' (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026). Pain owner is Suvi Lehtonen (Senior Sourcing Manager) and her global sourcing team. The business case now being built is expected to quantify payback and savings, but no ROI figure or headcount/cycle-time metric has been stated yet. Moving to quantify the value is the current step (Next_Step__c 18 Jun: 'Has to build out Business Case').

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Suvi Lehtonen, Senior Sourcing Manager, Evaluator
Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026, speaker Suvi Lehtonen
Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026, attendees Suvi, Ossi, Priit, Arto
SF Next_Step__c 18 Jun 2026
champion.narrative: Champion is Suvi Lehtonen, Senior Sourcing Manager, leading the global sourcing team and driving procurement process development. Evidence of advocacy: (1) Suvi initiated the conversation at a Zycus event (Casper stated on 26 Mar: 'we met there, we had a great conversation around what you were thinking... and then we agreed that we should have this call'); (2) brought Ossi (Purchasing Manager) into discovery call because Ossi has a personal target to increase AI usage this year; (3) stated on 26 Mar: 'I am also developing the procurement processes... I'm very interested in the tools and how the tools could come to help develop our way of working'; (4) co-authoring the business case with Ossi to present to CSO (Next_Step__c 18 Jun: 'Has to build out Business Case wtih internal Champion'). Access to Economic Buyer: Suvi reports directly to the relevant stakeholder. Engagement: active as of 8 days ago (LastActivityDate 18 Jun 2026). Strength: strong — Suvi is driving the evaluation, has executive access, and is building internal consensus across the category team (Ossi, Priit, Arto all participated in demo 17 Apr). No cooling signals; deal is progressing on her initiative.

sources: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026, speaker Suvi Lehtonen
Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026
competition.narrative: No competitor named in any Salesforce field (Competitors__c unknown) or on either Avoma call. Suvi mentioned on 26 Mar that they have an existing e-sourcing tool (later identified on 17 Apr as Scanmarket, used for critical suppliers ~10% of base) and are building in-house spend analytics, but neither was positioned as a competitive alternative to Zycus — both were described as gaps: 'e-sourcing tool provides basic templates but lacks AI capabilities' and 'in-house spend analytics near completion but offers no direct integration for automated reporting' (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026). No RFP, RFI, or mention of evaluating other vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer). This appears to be a sole-source evaluation driven by Suvi's initiative at the Zycus event, not a competitive bake-off. The gap: no explicit confirmation that other vendors are or are not in consideration; if this is truly sole-sourced, the business case and CSO sign-off are the only gates. Recommended move: confirm with Suvi whether other vendors will be invited before the CSO presentation.

sources: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026, speakers Suvi Lehtonen and Ossi Heikkinen
identify_pain.narrative: Pain confirmed across operational inefficiency and strategic visibility. Operational: Suvi stated on 26 Mar: 'Category reports built manually via Excel and PowerPoint, causing inefficiencies and version control issues... we don't even get it readily into Powerpoint format... it's print screens or taking Excels out and building the crafts ourselves... I keep on pushing the team that you have to do more, do it faster... I need all those sevens by tomorrow' (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026). Ossi added: 'if we send some report out and we have put the numbers in last week and this week, we will present that... it's one week old, and everything has been changed already... when you print something out from some system, then it's immediately old' (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026). Strategic: lack of AI-driven sourcing automation and real-time market intelligence; Suvi stated: 'e-sourcing tool provides basic templates but lacks AI capabilities, requiring manual setup and event management' and 'in-house spend analytics near completion but offers no direct integration for automated reporting' (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026). Pain owner is Suvi Lehtonen and the global sourcing team (Ossi, Priit, Arto). Compelling event: personal target set by Suvi to increase AI usage in procurement this year; CSO actively building AI agents and leadership receiving AI mentoring; budget decision planned for after summer 2026.

sources: Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026, speaker Suvi Lehtonen
Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026, speaker Casper Hoeholt
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics not yet scoped. Legal team has no contract tool today; redlining is done manually (Suvi asked on 17 Apr: 'is your legal team using any tool for this today, or are they using Sharepoint where they have all the redlining stored in?' — answer: manual process, source: Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026). No MSA, SOW, or InfoSec review has been discussed. No SI or partner involvement mentioned. No contract expiration forcing function (Current_Contract_Expiration__c not populated). Implementation scoping has not started; Zycus offered POC environment on 17 Apr, and Casper stated: 'Provision a proof-of-concept Zycus environment and send access credentials to Olvi team for testing' (source: Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026). Paper process will activate after CSO approves the business case (post-summer 2026). Gap: no visibility into legal/InfoSec timeline, approval chain for contract execution, or implementation partner requirements.

sources: Account-level contact: Pia Hortling, Group Chief Sustainability and Sourcing Officer
Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026, speaker Suvi Lehtonen
Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026, speaker Casper Hoeholt (Zycus)
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is Pia Hortling, Group Chief Sustainability and Sourcing Officer (CSO), identified by title and role but not yet directly engaged on this opportunity. Suvi reports to Pia and stated on 26 Mar: 'CSO actively supports AI initiatives, building internal agents and serving as pilot user for Copilot license... entire leadership team receiving AI mentoring' — Pia is the executive sponsor and budget authority. Casper (Zycus) suggested on 17 Apr: 'Plan to involve CSO in the upcoming system demo to secure executive alignment' (source: Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026). Pia has not appeared on any call to date; her sponsorship is passive/supportive but not yet active in the deal. The business case Suvi and Ossi are building is intended for Pia's approval after summer (Jul-Aug 2026). Budget sign-off will come from the relevant stakeholder: CSO has not been in the room; securing her direct engagement and approval of the business case is the next gate.

sources: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026, speaker Suvi Lehtonen
Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026, speaker Casper Hoeholt
SF Next_Step__c 18 Jun 2026
decision_process.narrative: Process read from call sequence: (1) Discovery call 26 Mar with Suvi (Senior Sourcing Manager) and Ossi (Purchasing Manager); (2) full demo 17 Apr with Suvi, Ossi, Priit (category manager), Arto (trainee) — multi-person buying committee confirmed; (3) POC environment approved on 17 Apr for hands-on testing; (4) business case development in progress (Next_Step__c 18 Jun); (5) planned presentation to CSO (Economic Buyer) after summer (Jul-Aug 2026) for budget approval. Suvi stated on 26 Mar: 'we probably don't have a budget at all... if we are in good way after the summer, then we may propose something to our budget' and 'we need to build out a business case, maybe together with Ossi, that you need to present to your manager ' (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026). Casper confirmed on 17 Apr: 'Next steps include gathering internal user counts, defining power vs. business user roles, and co-authoring a value assessment/ROI business case with Zycus' (source: Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026). Approval chain: Suvi/Ossi → CSO Pia Hortling → budget committee. No procurement or legal gate mentioned yet; contracting will follow budget approval.

sources: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026, speakers Suvi Lehtonen and Ossi Heikkinen
Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026, speakers Ossi Heikkinen and Suvi Lehtonen
decision_criteria.narrative: Evaluation criteria articulated across both calls: (1) AI-driven autonomous sourcing to automate RFP setup, Q&A, and bid evaluation; (2) real-time spend analytics with AI dashboards replacing manual PowerPoint/Excel reports; (3) integration of live external market feeds (LME aluminum, oil, gas, electricity) for benchmarking and forecasting; (4) guided buying (Merlin Intake) for indirect procurement to offload business-user queries; (5) supplier risk and ESG monitoring integrated with existing Zedex subscription; (6) contract repository and redlining automation (though legal team has no tool today). Ossi stressed on 17 Apr: 'integration of live market feeds (e.g., LME aluminum, oil, gas, electricity) for real-time benchmarking and forecasting' and 'can we do that that it will take something from somewhere and add that to the report if we prompt that in proper way?' (source: Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026). Suvi prioritized on 26 Mar: 'autonomous sourcing and Merlin insights to automate event creation, data analysis and market-driven prompts' and 'real-time dashboards or PDF outputs with preset filters' (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026). Fit criteria met: Zycus demo covered all six requirements; POC environment approved for hands-on validation.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Strong dual champion (Suvi, Senior Sourcing Manager reporting to the CSO) with pain, decision process and criteria confirmed and AI as the core wedge; no named competitor beyond a small Scanmarket footprint
Still Qualified stage (capped) with only a partial EB and the business case not yet built
Demo done (Apr) and the champion is building the internal business case
No call in ~11 weeks and close pushed out 76 days

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS fields not populated in Salesforce, but Avoma calls confirm AI Hungry tier. Suvi stated on 26 Mar: 'personal target has been set to increase AI usage in procurement processes this year and explore ready-to-use market solutions' and 'CSO actively supports AI initiatives, building internal agents... entire leadership team receiving AI mentoring to drive adoption... we have the co pilot license, the extra package, already for couple of years, and our team has been the pilot users' (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026). Company has dedicated in-house AI team; CSO is building her own AI agents; forward AI view across leadership. This is an AI-first evaluation; Zycus was engaged specifically for AI capabilities (autonomous sourcing, Merlin insights). Tier: AI Hungry.

Both actively engaged as of 8 days ago (LastActivityDate 18 Jun). Multi-threaded: Priit (category manager) and Arto (trainee) participated in demo 17 Apr. No cooling signals.

items.expected_effect: Tangible proof (live event or dashboard) will be far more compelling than slides in the CSO business case; activating the POC before summer break ensures momentum and prevents the evaluation from going cold in Jul-Aug

items.action: Confirm with Suvi whether other vendors will be invited before the CSO presentation
items.trigger: No competitor named on any call, but also no explicit confirmation this is sole-sourced; risk that a formal RFP extends timeline 6-12 months
items.expected_effect: If sole-source, the business case is the only gate and close date 31 Dec 2026 is credible; if RFP is planned, reset expectations and timeline to mid-2027

items.trigger: Zycus committed on 26 Mar to 'coordinate with the internal value team to develop a value dashboard and presentation using Olvi's data to support the business case after the demo'; demo was 17 Apr (~10 weeks ago)
items.expected_effect: Quantified ROI (savings, headcount, cycle-time) will strengthen the business case Suvi and Ossi present to CSO in Jul-Aug; without it, CSO approval may stall on unproven value

items.action: Secure CSO (Pia Hortling) attendance at a 30-minute AI-transformation executive briefing before she receives the business case

items.expected_effect: User counts are required to finalize pricing for the business case; without them, the ROI model is incomplete and CSO approval may be delayed

items: Champion Suvi Lehtonen initiated evaluation at Zycus event, brought Ossi (co-champion with personal AI target) into discovery call 26 Mar, drove full demo 17 Apr with multi-person team, and is co-authoring business case as of 18 Jun (8 days ago) — active and engaged
CSO (Economic Buyer) actively building AI agents and sponsoring leadership AI mentoring program — AI adoption is executive mandate, not grassroots experiment
POC environment approved 17 Apr for hands-on validation — buyer committed to technical proof, not just slides
Multi-threaded: 4 buyer participants across discovery and demo (Suvi, Ossi, Priit, Arto); buying committee confirmed
Business case in progress with internal champion (Next_Step__c 18 Jun) — next milestone is CSO presentation after summer (Jul-Aug 2026), on pace for late-2026 close
confidence_signals.summary: Strong forward signals: champion actively building business case (as of 8 days ago), POC environment approved, multi-threaded buying committee (Suvi, Ossi, Priit, Arto), executive AI mandate (CSO building AI agents, leadership AI mentoring), AI adoption is personal target for champion. No cooling signals; last activity 18 Jun 2026 (8 days ago). Deal is live and progressing on pace for late-2026 close.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 27 Mar 2026 (~3 months ago), close date 31 Dec 2026 (188 days out). Discovery call 26 Mar (~3 months ago), full demo 17 Apr (~10 weeks ago), last Salesforce activity 18 Jun (8 days ago). Time in Qualified stage ~3 months. Pace is consistent with 12-15 month enterprise motion; next milestone is business case presentation to CSO after summer (Jul-Aug 2026). No slip required; date is credible.
evidence: Discovery call 26 Mar 2026 (~3 months ago): Suvi Lehtonen articulated personal target to increase AI usage in procurement this year, exploring ready-to-use market solutions; primary interest in autonomous sourcing and Merlin insights to automate event creation, data analysis, and market-driven prompts (source: Avoma discovery call 26 Mar 2026)

Demo session 17 Apr 2026 (~10 weeks ago): Zycus delivered 1.5-hour demo covering sourcing, spend analytics, risk, and contract modules; Ossi stressed integration of live market feeds (LME aluminum, oil, gas, electricity) for real-time benchmarking; agreement reached to spin up free proof-of-concept environment for hands-on testing of autonomous sourcing and AI-driven tail-spend features (source: Avoma demo call 17 Apr 2026)

Next step 18 Jun 2026 (8 days ago, last activity): 'Has to build out Business Case wtih internal Champion' — Suvi and Ossi co-developing value case with internal champion to present to CSO after summer (source: SF Next_Step__c, LastActivityDate 18 Jun 2026)

Forecast Pipeline with $150k ACV is conservative: buyer has personal AI adoption target, executive AI mentoring program in place, CSO actively building AI agents, and Zycus POC environment approved; deal is live and progressing but not yet in proposal stage — Best Case is defensible
No close-date slip or stall signals; buyer engagement is consistent with enterprise discovery-to-proposal motion
north_star_verdict.headline: Champion building business case with CSO presentation planned post-summer, but no concrete meeting scheduled and eight days since last activity. Summer holiday period creates risk that momentum stalls before business case presentation materializes.

flags: Secure direct CSO (Pia Hortling, Economic Buyer) engagement before the business case is finalized — her active sponsorship on a call will validate budget authority and accelerate post-summer approval (current: passive/supportive but not yet in the room; source: no CSO appearance on calls 26 Mar or 17 Apr, Suvi building case to present to her)
best_practice_check.summary: Strong champion and multi-threaded discovery, but CSO (Economic Buyer) not yet directly engaged; business case in progress; sole-source status unconfirmed

competitive_position.summary: No named competitor. Scanmarket (incumbent e-sourcing tool for critical suppliers, ~10% of base) and in-house spend analytics are not positioned as alternatives; both described as capability gaps. This appears to be a sole-source evaluation. Threat level: none identified. Wedge: Zycus AI-driven automation (autonomous sourcing, Merlin insights, real-time dashboards) vs manual/non-AI incumbents. Risk: if other procurement platforms (Coupa, Ariba, Ivalua, GEP) are invited into a formal RFP before CSO sign-off, the deal extends 6-12 months and pricing pressure increases. Recommended move: confirm sole-source status with Suvi before the business case goes to CSO.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI narrative is the core wedge and strongly positioned. Suvi stated personal target to increase AI usage in procurement this year; CSO actively building AI agents; entire leadership team receiving AI mentoring; company has forward AI view. Zycus AI capabilities (autonomous sourcing, Merlin insights, real-time AI dashboards) directly address stated pain (manual reporting, no AI in e-sourcing). Demo 17 Apr covered agentic AI, Merlin Intake, and AI-driven tail-spend automation. POC approved for hands-on validation. No under-positioning risk; AI is the reason Suvi initiated this evaluation.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit confirmed across all six stated criteria. Zycus demo 17 Apr covered autonomous sourcing (RFP automation, Q&A, bid evaluation), real-time spend analytics with AI dashboards, live market feed integration (LME, oil, gas, electricity), guided buying (Merlin Intake), supplier risk/ESG monitoring (Zedex integration), and contract repository/redlining. POC environment approved for hands-on validation. No exposed gaps or misalignment surfaced.

Commercial intent shown—'How much does it cost?'—plus a free POC offer and a value/ROI workstream. Two transcribed calls support high confidence.
